user.title: Device Sentinel
user.description: Device Sentinel installs with working defaults and starts watching immediately. Tuning lives in the options after setup.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Device Sentinel is already configured. Only a single instance is supported.

init.title: Device Sentinel tuning
init.description:
Everything here is optional: Device Sentinel runs on sensible defaults from the moment it is installed. Work top to bottom when you do configure.

**Exclusions**: choose what is never judged or reported. Excluded items keep learning, so undoing costs nothing.

**Battery**: the low threshold, and which batteries are watched (exclude phones and tablets here).

**Notifications**: where alerts will go, quiet hours, and the daily reminder. Stored now, used once detection notifications arrive.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description:
Two target lists. High-priority targets are for phones that should always be reached: they bypass Do Not Disturb and quiet hours. Normal-priority targets get standard delivery and are held during quiet hours. Either list may be empty, and a target in both is treated as high. These settings are stored now and used once detection notifications are built; nothing is sent yet.

Full documentation: {wiki_link}

data_description.high_priority_targets: Notify targets that receive high-priority pushes. High priority bypasses Do Not Disturb and pierces quiet hours. Leave empty for none.
data_description.normal_priority_targets: Notify targets that receive normal pushes. Standard delivery, held during quiet hours. A target in both lists is treated as high. Leave empty for none.
data_description.persistent_enabled: Also maintain a persistent notification in the UI, replaced in place so it never stacks.
data_description.quiet_hours_enabled: When on, normal-priority pushes are held during the window below; high-priority targets still fire, and the lists and state keep updating.
data_description.quiet_hours_start: When the quiet window begins. A start later than the end crosses midnight.
data_description.quiet_hours_end: When the quiet window ends. Nothing fires at this time; it only defines the window.
data_description.daily_reminder_mode: None: notify only on new items. Overnight changes: only if something appeared during quiet hours. Daily summary: every day, with an all-clear when nothing is flagged.
data_description.daily_reminder_time: When the daily send is owed. The time is only a wake-up; a send missed during a reboot is caught up on the next run.

exclusions.title: Exclusions
exclusions.description:
Exclusion suppresses judgment, not observation: excluded devices and entities are never reported, but they keep their clocks and learned statistics, so undoing an exclusion is instant and nothing is lost. An excluded entity still counts as activity for its device. Excluding an integration catches only devices it owns, never hardware it merely sees (a router tracking a camera does not drag the camera with it).

Full documentation: {wiki_link}

data_description.excluded_entities: These entities are never reported themselves, but still count as activity for their device.
data_description.excluded_devices: These devices are never judged or listed.
data_description.excluded_labels: Devices and entities carrying these labels are excluded.
data_description.excluded_areas: Devices and entities in these areas are excluded.
data_description.excluded_integrations: Devices owned by these integrations are excluded. Hardware an integration merely sees (a router tracking a camera) is not caught.

battery.title: Battery

data.battery_excluded_integrations: Exclude whole integrations from battery detection

data_description.low_threshold: A battery at or below this level is flagged low. It clears once it climbs 2 points above the threshold.
data_description.battery_excluded_devices: Every listed device has a tracked battery. Checked devices are excluded from battery judgment only; everything else about them stays watched.
data_description.battery_excluded_integrations: One tick covers every battery that integration owns, present and future (phones under mobile_app, for example).

battery.description:
Every battery Device Sentinel finds is watched by default. Where a device has both a percentage battery and a low-battery flag, the percentage is used. A battery is flagged at or below the threshold and clears once it climbs 2 points above it, so a cell at the line never flaps, and the time it crossed is kept across restarts. Exclude the batteries you do not care about below; everything else about those devices stays watched.

Full documentation: {wiki_link}

options.overnight: Overnight changes (only if something was added during quiet hours)
options.daily: Daily summary (every day, all-clear when nothing is flagged)
